purpose: Answer questions about rituals by combining (a) ritual definitions from rituals_master, (b) character-known rituals from character_rituals, and (c) general ritual rules/constraints from the Rules database.

primary.name: rituals_master
primary.role: Canonical ritual definitions (what a ritual is).
primary.notes: Unique key is (type, level, name). If you later add a slug column, use slug as the stable lookup key.

primary.name: character_rituals
primary.notes: Prefer ritual_id FK to rituals_master.id. If ritual_id is missing, fall back to (ritual_name, ritual_type, level) matching.

secondary.name: rules_db
secondary.role: General rules about rituals (learning, casting constraints, costs, limits, clarifications).
secondary.notes: Do not duplicate global rules into rituals_master rows; attach relevant rule snippets at response time.

functions.name: get_ritual
functions.description: Fetch a single ritual by stable key and return canonical definition plus relevant global rules.
sql_examples: SELECT * FROM rituals_master WHERE type = ? AND level = ? AND name = ?;
rules_query_examples: search rules_db for: ritual name + "ritual" (only if needed)

functions.name: list_rituals
functions.description: List rituals with filters, used for browsing and UI lists.
sql_examples: SELECT id, name, type, level, description, source FROM rituals_master WHERE type = ? AND level BETWEEN ? AND ? ORDER BY level, name LIMIT ? OFFSET ?;
SELECT id, name, type, level, description FROM rituals_master WHERE ingredients LIKE CONCAT('%', ?, '%') ORDER BY type, level, name LIMIT ? OFFSET ?;

functions.name: get_character_rituals
functions.description: Return what a character knows, joined to the canonical ritual definitions.
sql_examples: SELECT cr.character_id, rm.* FROM character_rituals cr JOIN rituals_master rm ON rm.id = cr.ritual_id WHERE cr.character_id = ? ORDER BY rm.type, rm.level, rm.name;

SELECT cr.character_id, rm.* FROM character_rituals cr JOIN rituals_master rm ON rm.type = cr.ritual_type AND rm.level = cr.level AND rm.name = cr.ritual_name WHERE cr.character_id = ? ORDER BY rm.type, rm.level, rm.name;
rules_query_examples: If include_rules=true, attach rules for each involved ritual type (unique types) plus any global ritual rules.

functions.name: get_ritual_rules
functions.description: Retrieve general rules about rituals from the Rules database, optionally scoped to a tradition.
rules_query_examples: global: search rules_db for "ritual" AND ("learning" OR "casting" OR "components" OR "ingredients")
type: search rules_db for type + ("ritual" OR "rituals" OR "casting" OR "learning")

functions.name: can_character_cast_ritual
functions.description: Decision helper: check if character plausibly meets requirements to cast the ritual, using known character state + rules DB guidance. Returns 'unknown' if the system can't fully validate due to missing data.
behavior_notes: If character does not know the ritual (not in character_rituals), result=no.
If ritual requirements mention prerequisites not stored in DB (e.g., specific Path rating, mentors, special components), result=unknown with missing_data listed.
Attach relevant rules_db items explaining how prerequisites work.

key_principles: rituals_master is canonical definition; never store global casting rules in each ritual row.
character_rituals is character state; keep it minimal.
Rules DB is authoritative for general constraints and edge cases; attach rule snippets at runtime.
Prefer ritual_id foreign key joins; keep name/type/level fallback only for migration/backward compatibility.
suggested_future_migrations: Add rituals_master.slug VARCHAR(96) UNIQUE for stable lookups.
Add character_rituals.ritual_id FK and backfill; later deprecate ritual_name/ritual_type/level fields.
Optionally add FULLTEXT index on rituals_master (name, description, system_text, requirements, ingredients, source) for fast search.
